description_long: This is a large, rectangular stone block with a flat top and slightly beveled edges, showing signs of age and wear. The block is made from a single piece of stone, with a muted, earthy color and a rough, matte finish. On one of the shorter faces, there are visible engravings or inscriptions, possibly of historical or decorative significance. The longer sides display faint, linear patterns that may be remnants of ancient carvings or weathering effects. The block's corners are slightly rounded, and two protruding nubs are present on each short end, suggesting it may have been designed for handling or alignment. Its substantial mass and solid construction indicate it was likely used as a barrier or structural element in an outdoor or public setting.
description: A large, rectangular stone block with engravings on one end, faint linear patterns on the sides, and rounded corners with protruding nubs. The surface is rough, matte, and shows signs of age and weathering.
description_view_0: Front view showing the short face with visible engravings and two rounded nubs protruding from the top edge.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the long, flat surface with faint linear patterns and slightly rounded corners.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, with two rounded nubs but no visible engravings.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the long, flat surface with faint patterns and a more weathered appearance.
attribute_location_context: historic urban plaza or ancient city street
attribute_installation_method: set directly on compacted earth or stone foundation
attribute_weathering_pattern: Surface shows signs of erosion and discoloration, with some rounded edges and faint linear weathering marks.
attribute_presence_of_markings_or_engrav: Visible engravings or inscriptions on one short face, with faint linear patterns on the long sides.
